# Exodus Chapter 34 — The LOGOS Bible (Formal)
**Translator:** LOGOS | **Source:** Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 104–106; Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 77–78 | **Source Images:** `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_034_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_034_page_002.jpg`, `source-texts/hebrew-OT/EXO_034_page_003.jpg`; `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_034_vaticanus_page_001.jpg`, `source-texts/greek-OT-LXX/EXO_034_vaticanus_page_002.jpg`

---

## New Tablets and the Command to Ascend (vv. 1–4)

**1** And YHWH said [**vayyomer YHWH**] to [**el**] Moses [**Mosheh**]: "Hew [**pesal**] for yourself [**lekha**] two [**sheney**] tablets of [**luchot**] stone [**avanim**] like the first ones [**karishonim**], and I will write [**vekhatavti**] upon [**al**] the tablets [**halluchot**] the words [**et-haddevarim**] which [**asher**] were [**hayu**] upon [**al**] the first [**halluchot harishonim**] tablets [**asher**] which [**asher**] you shattered [**shibbarta**]."

**2** "And be prepared [**veheyeh nakhon**] for the morning [**labboqer**], and go up [**ve'alita**] in the morning [**vabboqer**] to [**el**] Mount [**har**] Sinai [**Sinay**], and station yourself [**venitsavta**] there [**sham**] for Me [**li**] on [**al**] the top of [**rosh**] the mountain [**hahar**]."

**3** "And no [**velo**] man [**ish**] shall go up [**ya'aleh**] with you [**immakh**], and also [**vegam**] no [**al**] man [**ish**] shall be seen [**yera'eh**] in all [**bekhol**] the mountain [**hahar**]; also [**gam**] the flocks [**hatson**] and the cattle [**habaqar**] shall not [**al**] graze [**yir'u**] before [**el-mul**] that [**hahu**] mountain [**hahar**]."

**4** And he hewed [**vayyifsol**] two [**sheney**] tablets of [**luchot**] stone [**avanim**] like the first ones [**karishonim**], and Moses rose early [**vayyashkem Mosheh**] in the morning [**babboqer**] and went up [**vayyaal**] to [**el**] Mount [**har**] Sinai [**Sinay**], as [**ka'asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] had commanded him [**tsivvah oto**], and he took [**vayyiqqach**] in his hand [**beyado**] two [**sheney**] tablets of [**luchot**] stone [**avanim**].

---

## YHWH Descends in the Cloud: The Proclamation of the Name (vv. 5–9)

**5** And YHWH descended [**vayyered YHWH**] in the cloud [**be'anan**], and he stationed himself [**vayyityatsev**] with Him [**immo**] there [**sham**], and he proclaimed [**vayyiqra**] the name of [**veshem**] YHWH [**YHWH**].

**6** And YHWH passed [**vayyaavor YHWH**] before his face [**al-panav**] and proclaimed [**vayyiqra**]: "YHWH [**YHWH**], YHWH [**YHWH**], God [**El**] compassionate [**rachum**] and gracious [**vechannun**], slow [**erekh**] of anger [**appayim**] and great [**verav**] of steadfast love [**chesed**] and faithfulness [**ve'emet**],

**7** keeping [**notser**] steadfast love [**chesed**] for thousands [**la'alafim**], forgiving [**nose**] iniquity [**avon**] and transgression [**vafesha**] and sin [**vechatta'ah**], and acquitting he will surely not acquit [**venaqeh lo yenaqqeh**], visiting [**poqed**] the iniquity of [**avon**] fathers [**avot**] upon [**al**] sons [**banim**] and upon [**ve'al**] sons' sons [**beney vanim**], upon [**al**] the third generation [**shilleshim**] and upon [**ve'al**] the fourth generation [**ribbe'im**]."

**8** And Moses hastened [**vayemaher Mosheh**] and bowed his head [**vayyiqqod**] toward the ground [**artsah**] and bowed down [**vayyishtachu**].

**9** And he said [**vayyomer**]: "If [**im**] now [**na**] I have found [**matsati**] favor [**chen**] in Your eyes [**be'eynekha**], O Lord [**Adonay**], let the Lord [**Adonay**] go [**yelekh**], I pray [**na**], in our midst [**beqirbenu**]; for [**ki**] it is a stiff-necked [**qesheh-oref**] people [**am**], and You shall forgive [**vesalachta**] our iniquity [**la'avonenu**] and our sin [**ulechattatennu**], and You shall take us as an inheritance [**unchalttanu**]."

---

## The Covenant Renewed: YHWH's Terms (vv. 10–16)

**10** And He said [**vayyomer**]: "Behold [**hinneh**], I [**anokhi**] am cutting [**koret**] a covenant [**berit**]. Before [**neged**] all [**kol**] your people [**ammekha**] I will do [**e'eseh**] wonders [**nifla'ot**] which [**asher**] have not [**lo**] been created [**nivre'u**] in all [**bekhol**] the earth [**ha'arets**] and in all [**uvekhol**] the nations [**haggoyim**]; and all [**vekhol**] the people [**ha'am**] which [**asher**] you [**attah**] are in its midst [**beqirbo**] shall see [**ra'u**] the work of [**et-ma'aseh**] YHWH [**YHWH**], for [**ki**] it is an awesome thing [**nora**] that [**asher**] I [**ani**] am doing [**oseh**] with you [**immakh**].

**11** "Keep [**shemor**] for yourself [**lekha**] that which [**et asher**] I [**anokhi**] am commanding you [**metsavvekha**] today [**hayyom**]. Behold [**hinneni**], I am driving out [**goresh**] before you [**mippanekha**] the Amorite [**et-ha'Emori**] and the Canaanite [**vehaKena'ani**] and the Hittite [**vehaChitti**] and the Perizzite [**vehaPerizzi**] and the Hivite [**vehaChivvi**] and the Jebusite [**vehaYevusi**].

**12** "Guard yourself [**hishamer lekha**] lest [**pen**] you cut [**tikhrot**] a covenant [**berit**] with the inhabitant of [**leyoshev**] the land [**ha'arets**] upon [**al**] which [**asher**] you [**attah**] are coming [**ba**] to it [**aleha**], lest [**pen**] it become [**yihyeh**] a snare [**lemoqesh**] in your midst [**beqirbekha**].

**13** "For [**ki**] their altars [**et-mizbechotam**] you shall tear down [**tittotsu**], and their pillars [**et-mattsevotam**] you shall shatter [**teshabberun**], and their Asherah poles [**et-asherav**] you shall cut down [**tikhrotun**].

**14** "For [**ki**] you shall not [**lo**] bow down [**tishtachaveh**] to [**le**] another [**acher**] god [**el**], for [**ki**] YHWH [**YHWH**], Jealous [**Qanna**] is His name [**shemo**] — a jealous [**qanna**] God [**El**] He is [**hu**].

**15** "Lest [**pen**] you cut [**tikhrot**] a covenant [**berit**] with the inhabitant of [**leyoshev**] the land [**ha'arets**], and they prostitute themselves [**vezanu**] after [**acharey**] their gods [**eloheyhem**], and they sacrifice [**vezavechu**] to their gods [**le'eloheyhem**], and one calls [**veqara**] to you [**lekha**] and you eat [**ve'akhalta**] from his sacrifice [**mizzivcho**],

**16** "and you take [**velaqachta**] from his daughters [**mibbnotav**] for your sons [**levanekha**], and his daughters prostitute themselves [**vezanu venotav**] after [**acharey**] their gods [**eloheyhen**], and they cause your sons to prostitute themselves [**vehiznu et-banekha**] after [**acharey**] their gods [**eloheyhen**].

---

## The Festival Laws: Unleavened Bread, Firstborn, Sabbath, Weeks, Ingathering (vv. 17–26)

**17** "Molten [**massekah**] gods [**elohey**] you shall not [**lo**] make [**ta'aseh**] for yourself [**lakh**].

**18** "The Feast of [**chag**] Unleavened Bread [**hamMatsot**] you shall keep [**tishmor**]. Seven [**shiv'at**] days [**yamim**] you shall eat [**tokhal**] unleavened bread [**matsot**], as [**ka'asher**] I commanded you [**tsivvitikha**], at the appointed time of [**lemo'ed**] the month of [**chodesh**] the Aviv [**ha'Aviv**], for [**ki**] in the month of [**bechodesh**] the Aviv [**ha'Aviv**] you went out [**yatsata**] from Egypt [**miMitsrayim**].

**19** "Every [**kol**] opener of [**peter**] a womb [**rechem**] is Mine [**li**], and all [**vekhol**] your livestock [**miqnekha**] that is male [**tizzakhar**], the opener of [**peter**] ox [**shor**] and sheep [**vaseh**].

**20** "And the opener of [**ufeter**] a donkey [**chamor**] you shall redeem [**tifdeh**] with a lamb [**beseh**], and if [**ve'im**] you do not [**lo**] redeem it [**tifdeh**], then you shall break its neck [**va'arafto**]. Every [**kol**] firstborn of [**bekhor**] your sons [**banekha**] you shall redeem [**tifdeh**], and they shall not [**velo**] appear [**yera'u**] before Me [**fanay**] empty-handed [**reyqam**].

**21** "Six [**sheshet**] days [**yamim**] you shall work [**ta'avod**], and on the seventh [**uvayom hashevi'i**] day [**tishbot**] you shall rest [**tishbot**]; in plowing time [**becharish**] and in harvest [**uvaqatsir**] you shall rest [**tishbot**].

**22** "And the Feast of [**vechag**] Weeks [**Shavu'ot**] you shall observe [**ta'aseh lekha**] — the firstfruits of [**bikkurey**] the wheat [**chittim**] harvest [**qetsir**] — and the Feast of [**vechag**] Ingathering [**ha'Asif**] at the turn of [**tequfat**] the year [**hashanah**].

**23** "Three [**shalosh**] times [**pe'amim**] in the year [**bashanah**] every [**kol**] male among you [**zekhurkhah**] shall appear [**yera'eh**] before [**et-peney**] the Lord [**ha'Adon**] YHWH [**YHWH**], God of [**Elohey**] Israel [**Yisra'el**].

**24** "For [**ki**] I will dispossess [**horish**] nations [**goyim**] before you [**mippanekha**], and I will enlarge [**vehirchavti**] your border [**et-gevulekha**]; and no [**velo**] man [**ish**] shall covet [**yachmod**] your land [**et-artsekha**] when you go up [**ba'alotekha**] to appear [**lera'ot**] before [**et-peney**] YHWH [**YHWH**] your God [**Elohekha**] three [**shalosh**] times [**pe'amim**] in the year [**bashanah**].

**25** "You shall not [**lo**] slaughter [**tishchat**] upon [**al**] leaven [**chamets**] the blood of [**dam**] My sacrifice [**zivchi**], and the sacrifice of [**vezevach**] the Feast of [**chag**] the Passover [**haPesach**] shall not [**lo**] remain overnight [**yalin**] until morning [**labboqer**].

**26** "The choicest [**reshit**] firstfruits of [**bikkurey**] your ground [**admatekha**] you shall bring [**tavi**] to the house of [**beyt**] YHWH [**YHWH**] your God [**Elohekha**]. You shall not [**lo**] boil [**tevashel**] a kid [**gedi**] in the milk of [**bachalev**] its mother [**immo**]."

---

## The Writing of the Covenant Words: Forty Days (vv. 27–28)

**27** And YHWH said [**vayyomer YHWH**] to [**el**] Moses [**Mosheh**]: "Write [**ketav**] for yourself [**lekha**] these [**et-haddevarim ha'elleh**] words [**haddevarim**], for [**ki**] according to [**al-pi**] the mouth of [**pi**] these [**haddevarim ha'elleh**] words [**haddevarim**] I have cut [**karati**] a covenant [**berit**] with you [**ittekha**] and with [**ve'et**] Israel [**Yisra'el**]."

**28** And he was [**vayehi**] there [**sham**] with [**im**] YHWH [**YHWH**] forty [**arba'im**] days [**yom**] and forty [**ve'arba'im**] nights [**laylah**]; bread [**lechem**] he did not [**lo**] eat [**akhal**], and water [**umayim**] he did not [**lo**] drink [**shatah**]. And he wrote [**vayyikhtov**] upon [**al**] the tablets [**halluchot**] the words of [**et-divrey**] the covenant [**habberit**], the Ten [**aseret**] Words [**haddevarim**].

---

## The Shining Face of Moses (vv. 29–35)

**29** And it was [**vayehi**], when Moses came down [**beredet Mosheh**] from Mount [**mehar**] Sinai [**Sinay**] — and the two [**usheney**] tablets of [**luchot**] the Testimony [**ha'edut**] were in the hand of [**beyad**] Moses [**Mosheh**] when he came down [**beridto**] from the mountain [**min-hahar**] — and Moses [**uMosheh**] did not [**lo**] know [**yada**] that [**ki**] the skin of [**or**] his face [**panav**] shone [**qaran**] when He spoke [**bedabbero**] with him [**itto**].

**30** And Aaron [**vayyar Aharon**] and all [**vekhol**] the Sons of Israel [**beney Yisra'el**] saw [**vayyir'u**] Moses [**et-Mosheh**], and behold [**vehinneh**], the skin of [**or**] his face [**panav**] shone [**qaran**], and they were afraid [**vayyire'u**] to draw near [**miggeshset**] to him [**elav**].

**31** And Moses called [**vayyiqra aleihem Mosheh**] to them, and Aaron [**vayyashuvu elav Aharon**] and all [**vekhol**] the leaders [**hannesi'im**] in the congregation [**ba'edah**] returned [**vayyashuvu**] to him, and Moses spoke [**vayedabber Mosheh**] to them [**aleihem**].

**32** And afterward [**ve'acharey-khen**] all [**kol**] the Sons of Israel [**beney Yisra'el**] drew near [**niggashu**], and he commanded them [**vayetsavvem**] all [**et kol**] that [**asher**] YHWH [**YHWH**] had spoken [**dibber**] with him [**itto**] on Mount [**behar**] Sinai [**Sinay**].

**33** And Moses finished [**vayekhal Mosheh**] speaking [**middabber**] with them [**ittam**], and he put [**vayyitten**] upon [**al**] his face [**panav**] a veil [**masveh**].

**34** And when Moses came [**uvevo Mosheh**] before [**lifney**] YHWH [**YHWH**] to speak [**ledabber**] with Him [**itto**], he would remove [**yasir**] the veil [**et-hammasveh**] until [**ad**] he went out [**tseto**]; and he would go out [**veyatsa**] and speak [**vedibber**] to [**el**] the Sons of Israel [**beney Yisra'el**] that which [**et asher**] he was commanded [**yetsuvveh**].

**35** And the Sons of Israel [**beney Yisra'el**] saw [**vera'u**] the face of [**et-peney**] Moses [**Mosheh**], that [**ki**] the skin of [**or**] the face of [**peney**] Moses [**Mosheh**] shone [**qaran**]; and Moses would put back [**veheshiv Mosheh**] the veil [**et-hammasveh**] upon [**al**] his face [**panav**] until [**ad**] he went in [**bo'o**] to speak [**ledabber**] with Him [**itto**].

---

## LOGOS Translation Notes

### Manuscript Observations

1. **Leningrad Codex (B19A), pp. 104–106:** The text of Exodus 34 begins on folio 104 (page 1 image), continuing from the close of chapter 33 in the right-hand column. The parashah setumah mark is visible before v. 1. The chapter extends across three folios, with the divine proclamation (vv. 6–7) prominently centered in the text block on page 104. A masorah parva note appears beside v. 6 at the divine attributes passage. The festival laws (vv. 18–26) occupy the central and left columns of page 105, and the shining-face narrative (vv. 29–35) concludes on page 106. Vowel pointing and cantillation marks are intact throughout; the Masoretic accentuation on v. 6 places the atnach (major disjunctive) after "ve'emet," dividing the proclamation into two balanced halves.

2. **Codex Vaticanus (Vat.gr.1209), pp. 77–78:** The Greek text of Exodus 34 (LXX chapter heading KZ visible at the top of page 77) runs across both pages in the standard three-column uncial format. The scriptio continua is unbroken. The Vaticanus text closely follows the MT verse structure. Notable divergence: in v. 28, Vaticanus assigns the writing of the tablets to God ("egrapse" without explicit subject change), whereas the MT's subject is ambiguous (Moses or YHWH). The LXX also expands v. 29 with "katabainontos autou ek tou orous" (as he was descending from the mountain), making the temporal clause more explicit. The term for Moses' shining face in the LXX is "dedoxastai" (was glorified), rather than a horn/ray image, reflecting an interpretive choice that avoids the ambiguity of the Hebrew qaran.

### Key Translation Decisions

3. **v. 1 — "pesal lekha" (hew for yourself):** The imperative "pesal" derives from the root פסל (pasal), meaning to hew or carve stone. The same root yields "pesel" (graven image), creating an ironic wordplay: Moses carves tablets while Israel is forbidden from carving images. Rendered "hew for yourself" to preserve the formal imperative. The LXX reads "laxeuson seauto" (hew for yourself), using a different verb root (laxeuo) but preserving the reflexive construction.

4. **v. 5 — Ambiguity of subject:** The MT reads "vayyityatsev immo sham vayyiqra veshem YHWH" — the subject of "he stationed himself" and "he proclaimed" is grammatically ambiguous (Moses or YHWH). The immediate context of v. 4 (Moses ascending) and v. 6 (YHWH passing by) creates deliberate ambiguity. This translation preserves the lower-case "he" for both verbs, maintaining the textual openness. The LXX resolves the ambiguity in favor of YHWH: "kai parestethe ekei Kurios" (and the Lord stood there).

5. **vv. 6–7 — The Thirteen Attributes (Middot haRachamim):** This is the most theologically significant passage in the chapter and one of the most cited in the entire Hebrew Bible. The formal structure is preserved exactly:
   - Two occurrences of the divine name: "YHWH, YHWH"
   - Five positive attributes: compassionate (rachum), gracious (channun), slow of anger (erekh appayim), great of steadfast love (rav chesed), and faithfulness (emet)
   - Three acts of forgiveness: forgiving iniquity (nose avon), transgression (pesha), and sin (chatta'ah)
   - The infinitive absolute construction: "venaqeh lo yenaqqeh" — rendered "acquitting he will surely not acquit" to preserve the doubling structure of the infinitive absolute followed by its finite form, conveying emphasis. This is the only negative attribute in the proclamation and serves as the hinge between mercy and justice.
   - Generational visitation: upon sons and sons' sons, upon the third and fourth generation.

6. **v. 7 — "venaqeh lo yenaqqeh" (infinitive absolute):** The infinitive absolute (naqeh) followed by the negated finite verb (lo yenaqqeh) creates a construction that literally reads "acquitting, he will not acquit." This preserves the Hebrew doubling convention specified in the binding conventions. The LXX renders "kai ou kathariei ton enokhon" (and he will not cleanse the guilty), losing the emphatic doubling but clarifying the legal sense.

7. **v. 9 — "unchalttanu" (and You shall take us as an inheritance):** From the root נחל (nachal), meaning to take as an inheritance or a possession. Moses asks YHWH to take Israel as His own hereditary possession. Rendered "You shall take us as an inheritance" to preserve the covenantal-inheritance sense. The LXX reads "kai esometha soi" (and we shall be yours), simplifying the inheritance metaphor.

8. **v. 14 — "YHWH Qanna shemo" (YHWH — Jealous is His name):** The word "qanna" (jealous/zealous) functions here as a divine title or name-attribute. The construction "X is His name" is a name-revelation formula (cf. Amos 4:13, 5:8). Rendered with "Jealous" capitalized to indicate its function as a proper title. The LXX reads "Kurios ho theos, zelotes onoma auto" (the Lord God, Zealous is His name).

9. **v. 19 — "kol peter rechem li" (every opener of a womb is Mine):** The term "peter" (opener) is a technical term for the firstborn that opens the womb. Preserved literally as "opener of a womb" rather than the smoother "firstborn." The phrase establishes YHWH's claim over all firstborn life.

10. **v. 20 — "va'arafto" (then you shall break its neck):** From the root ערף (araf), specifically referring to the breaking of the neck of an unredeemed firstborn donkey. This is a technical sacrificial/legal term. Rendered literally: "you shall break its neck."

11. **v. 22 — "Shavu'ot ... ha'Asif":** The two festival names are transliterated and preserved: "Weeks" (Shavu'ot) and "Ingathering" (ha'Asif). The Feast of Weeks is tied to the wheat harvest firstfruits; the Feast of Ingathering corresponds to the autumn harvest at the "turn of the year" (tequfat hashanah), the autumnal equinox.

12. **v. 28 — Subject ambiguity of "vayyikhtov":** The MT reads "vayyikhtov al-halluchot" (and he wrote upon the tablets). The nearest explicit subject is Moses (v. 28a: "he was there"), but v. 1 states that YHWH would write on the tablets. This deliberate ambiguity is preserved by retaining "he wrote" without specifying the subject beyond the pronoun. The LXX (Vaticanus) likewise uses "egrapse" without resolving the referent explicitly, though the broader LXX tradition tends to assign the writing to YHWH.

13. **v. 29 — "qaran or panav" (the skin of his face shone/sent forth rays):** The verb "qaran" (from qeren, horn/ray) is unique in the Hebrew Bible in this verbal form. It means to send forth rays or beams of light. The Vulgate's "cornuta esset facies" (his face was horned) reflects an alternative reading of qeren as "horn" — the source of Michelangelo's horned Moses. The LXX reads "dedoxastai he opsis tou chromatos tou prosopou autou" (the appearance of the complexion of his face was glorified), interpreting the radiance as divine glory rather than horns or rays. This translation renders "shone" as the most accurate English equivalent of the ray-sending verbal form.

14. **v. 33 — "masveh" (veil):** This noun appears only in Exodus 34:33–35 in the entire Hebrew Bible (hapax legomenon in its usage context). Its etymology is debated — possibly from the root סוה (savah, to cover or wrap). Rendered "veil" following standard convention. The LXX reads "kalumma" (covering/veil), from which Paul draws in 2 Corinthians 3:13–16.

15. **vv. 34–35 — Iterative verbal pattern:** As in Exodus 33:7–11, the verbs in vv. 34–35 shift to an iterative/habitual pattern, describing Moses' repeated practice of removing and replacing the veil. The imperfect forms "yasir" (would remove), "veyatsa" (would go out), "vedibber" (would speak), and "veheshiv" (would put back) convey an ongoing custom. This is rendered with habitual "would" phrasing to distinguish the repeated pattern from single-occurrence narrative.

### Theological Architecture of the Chapter

16. **The Covenant Renewal Pattern:** Exodus 34 mirrors and fulfills the structure of Exodus 19–24 (the original Sinai covenant). Both include: ascent to the mountain (34:4 // 19:20), theophany (34:5–7 // 19:16–19), covenant stipulations (34:11–26 // 20:1–23:19), and the writing of covenant terms (34:27–28 // 24:4, 12). The repetition signals that the broken covenant (ch. 32) has been genuinely restored, not merely patched.

17. **The Thirteen Attributes as the Heart of Torah:** The divine self-proclamation in vv. 6–7 is the theological center of the Exodus narrative and arguably the most important theological statement in the Hebrew Bible. It is cited or echoed in Numbers 14:18, Nehemiah 9:17, Psalms 86:15, 103:8, 145:8, Joel 2:13, Jonah 4:2, Nahum 1:3, and throughout the prophetic and wisdom literature. The tension between "great of steadfast love" and "acquitting he will surely not acquit" is not a contradiction but a statement of the full divine character — mercy and justice held together without resolution.

18. **The Shining Face and the Veil:** The qaran/radiance of Moses' face (vv. 29–35) forms the literary counterpart to the golden calf (ch. 32). Where the calf represented a false, manufactured representation of the divine, Moses' shining face is the authentic, unmanufactured effect of genuine encounter with YHWH. The veil functions not to protect Moses but to shield the people from the visible evidence of divine encounter — a continuation of the "cannot see My face and live" motif from 33:20.

19. **Formal Equivalence Methodology:** This translation maintains word-order fidelity to the Hebrew wherever English syntax permits, preserves all conjunctions (waw-consecutive, waw-copulative), retains the Hebrew clause structure including fronted objects and subjects, renders the infinitive absolute construction with doubling ("acquitting he will surely not acquit"), and provides consistent English glosses for each Hebrew lexeme. Proper names follow standard transliteration conventions (Sinai, not Sinay in final form; but the transliteration bracket preserves the Hebrew). YHWH is transliterated throughout per binding conventions. "Sons of Israel" is preserved rather than "Israelites" or "children of Israel."
